reviews.review_title: A truly epic Megaman style Platformer!
reviews.review_description:
If you have a love of classic platformers and want to experience that in VR this is definitely a game worth your time! It brings the classic megaman challenge to first person and makes you feel insanely immersed!

Also, many of the painful elements that made you wanna knock yourself out were improved in this game like checkpoints!

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking out the game! 🙏

reviews.review_description: The game is  terrible, and the soulless AI-generated intro is the highlight. It feels like you should be paid to play this.

developer_response.body: It’s a hard game and is definitely not for everyone. Sorry you did not like it. Maybe the challenge was a bit too much for you?

reviews.review_description:
Read the 5 star ratings, so bought the game in a great discount but I don't understand the hype. I'm 43, like to play oldscool games like Heroes 3, Fallout 1 & 2, but here I just can't find any artistic value. The best part of the game was the AI generated intro. After that rubbish textures, rubbish effects and so on.
I deleted it after 10 minutes. Fortunately bought it for 1$ and not like 10 or what was the original price.
Also don't understand the 3.6GB space requirements, looks like some unoptimalization too.

I want to start by saying that this is a good game. I know it was made by one person, and that's why I decided to support the developer and buy the game.

However! There are three problems in this game that significantly spoil the experience:

1. Sloppy graphics. I don't have complaints about the overall level of graphics; rather, it's about the lack of attention to detail. I constantly see textures sloppily applied to objects, which could be trimmed in the wrong place or stretched/flattened. Additionally, there are numerous shaders that strain the eyes. It's worth cleaning this up.

2. Dumb enemies. I understand that essentially, this is Megaman in VR, and enemies in Megaman were not known for their intelligence; they simply repeated some primitive patterns. Nevertheless, I often get tired of dealing with cyborgs running straight at me and flying gadgets appearing in different places.

3. The loading screen consisting of a bunch of repetitive, generated AI pictures. I have nothing against AI, but it could have been used to create a whole narrative comic instead of looking at a bunch of repetitive pictures.

If these issues are not addressed, the game is destined for niche status on the sidelines of the Apps Lab. And that's unfortunate.

PS Response to the developer: No, this is not good graphics. It's graphics made of mismatched textures, odd objects, and a huge amount of eye-straining shaders. You can't just take a good engine, turn on reflections, shaders, and other effects and say that it's good graphics. It needs to be consistent.

Also, I would like to say that if you admit you're just one person with no budget, it's quite strange to set a price for the game comparable to what whole development teams charge. I personally was ready to pay this price, but honestly, in its current state, the game is worth at most $5.

developer_response.body:
Hi, Thanks for playing the game I really appreciate the support! 🙏 It means a lot to me. I can try to address your issues below. 

1. The Graphics are good and look even better on the Quest 3 but I agree some textures do look low res and stretched in some places because the Quest 2 could barely handle the graphics and the levels are HUGE compared to most games, but the game itself is Overall pleasing to look at as long as you don't inspect every little wall/Floor for little details/ things. The levels are meant to run through fast and not stop and explore or look around except only to find hidden items. 

2. The Enemies AI is simple just like the Old School days they follow you and chase you while they shot and stand still and shoot and fly/teleport and shoot that's it, but I feel like it makes the game harder because they are literally programmed to kill and that's it! 

3. The AI images and story are because I'm a One Man team with no Budget so I needed help with this so I used AI to help me craft the images for the story. 

Sorry if those things ruined the experience for you but beyond those issues it is a solid Title with Super Fun Gameplay that you can't find anywhere else on the Meta Quest. Thanks for playing and I hope you get to play through the whole thing and beat the game.

reviews.review_description:
This game is lacking in every possible way. Even for a game in development. This feels more like a school project done in a single day. I don't like ratings game 1 star, so I gave 2.

Story
There is some sort of story with AI generated image showed in a slide show at the very start of the game.

Music
The most complex music must have about 4 different notes, which become annoying really fast. 

Graphics
Fairly ugly and with basically one unique texture (a different one for each level).

Enemies 
2 differents kind without any sort of AI.

Controls
They are mostly fine but hard to master for a fast-paced game. 

Weapons 
Awful aim and the shooting isn't satisfying at all. There's a clear lack of diversity too.

Bugs
They aren't hard to find.

Annoying stuff 
You have no feedback when getting hit. The holster doesn't turn when you do. The few weapons (aside from the 2 default ones), the shield, and the grenade, can't be holstered.

Pricing 
20€ ? Seriously ?

developer_response.body:
It sounds like your just trying to bash the game honestly. I assure you this was no School project 
Or done in one day this game took over a year of my life just to create. Let’s address everything you 
Mention below.

Story: super awesome save the planet from the evil Eye Organization 

Music: Super Awesome Old School Techno Sound tunes that will get stuck in your head.

Graphics: some of the best the quest has to offer each level is super unique and has waaay more than Just one texture/material .

Enemies: there are tons of enemies. Each level has different enemies cyborgs, robots, drones, spider robots 

Controls: are easy to learn and if they were hard  to master you might just need more practice.

Weapons: seriously?  Who wouldn’t want a Arm Cannon in VR? Not to mention the 
Pistol, sword, axe, spear, grenades, shields, and all the different types of blasters you unlock.

Bugs: there are not any I’m aware of.

Annoying stuff: only thing annoying about my game is your review.

Price: if your not willing to help support Indie VR Development your not a true VR supporter period. 
I poured my soul into this Game and it’s to bad you did not see that. Thanks for playing but do 
Know if you decide to never play it again you will only be missing out. Thanks

reviews.review_description:
This game has it all. Great graphics, good story and brings back the nostalgia with the graphic style and music.
If you like a challenge then this shooter is perfect for you. 
Great price for an incredible game

I would give it more stars if I could
It’s good, tough to get your head around - but that’s part of the fun!

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking it out! It’s definitely a challenging game but super rewarding when you pass a hard level!🦾

reviews.review_description:
Got to admit I do love the game but wish the


 various weapons were explained more,yes I can use gun and cannon etc and axe n sword n spear,but grenade took me 3 goes but other 2 ball type things I'm lost do I throw em ? Overall great game

Quick update just  opened game and wow really enjoyed the whole experience an amazing and challenging game taxes the brain and your aim

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! The ball things I think you’re referring to are grenade ammo. One is for your grenade launcher and one is for your shield grenade launcher. 🙂 the different blasters you unlock can be used by pressing the B button

reviews.review_description:
I read the reviews about it being like old school Metroid or mega man and thought I had to give it a try. 

I see 100 percent metroid in this game.  

I hope this is not the finished product and the devs keep working on it. It’s a solid game core wise. 

At 3 gigabytes I was expecting a bit more graphic wise. 

But for getting it on sale I cannot really complain, I just hope again the devs keep updating it. There is potential in this game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, I hope you play more and make it all the way to the end! And yes, there will definitely be more System Critical to come! 🦾

reviews.review_description: I find the game balanced, as long as you stay balanced haha. Has replay value and yes like many say its very much inspired and feels like megaman or metroid. The double jumping and platforming becomes easy to master once you play level 4 and 5. Maybe start on those. I look forward to this developers growth and future games.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! There definitely will be more to come for System Critical! 🦾

reviews.review_title: What If Mega Man Was A VR Game?
reviews.review_description:
If you were a fan of the old school Mega Man games, then System Critical 2 is a game for you. System Critical 2 is a game that plays like a platformer from the 90s. From the running and gunning, to the platform jumps that strikes fear deep inside of you. This game gives you a rush that I have not felt in a video game in very long time.

The game sports some amazing graphics with a retro sound track, that gives you an immersive world from the past. The controls and gameplay is spot on for some really fast action. The game is packing some of the toughest gameplay I have experienced in VR, and dyeing over and over is a given. Like the old school games, you can't just pause the game and save your progress before attempting a jump or taking on a boss. The game only saves after you have finished a level and only has check points to respawn at during your run. 

The game is broken down into 9 levels, each with a boss at the end to battle. I have only finished 4 of the levels after putting more then 3hrs into the game. If you are looking for a easy game where you can complete it fast, this is not the game for you. This game is for the people who want to face a challenge and you will need to put in the grind to master and complete the levels.

The part of the game I enjoyed the most is jumping from platform to platform. Some of the levels left my body shaking, and I had to take a break from playing to relax and pull myself together before moving on. I do not have a fear of heights in real life, but this game struck so much fear into me with some of the jumps. I even lost my balance a few times while playing it and all most tumbled over.

If you are like me and find most of todays games just don't pack a challenge and you can beat them in less then a week of game play, then try this game out for sure. Prepare for some sweat soak clothes from how intense this game is.

Just tried this game out… I’m not a platform, jumping fan… so this may show in my review.

The game is just ok… graphics n sound are just ok… nothing stands out.

Weapons are nothing special.

If you like run n gun, with platform jumping and lots of it then you may enjoy the game.

There’s a standard gun, and like an energy weapon that you can charge up.
You can get melee weapons that do extra damage if attack enemy from
behind.. but it’s just a case of run, dodge and jump your way to the boss at the end, reaching checkpoints.

There’s various levels to choose from to give variety. 
Some say it’s like Metroid, but Ive not played that as again, not my type of game

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking it out! It’s definitely not going to be for everybody given it’s high challenge level and hardness but it’s super fun and addictive once you get the hang of it! 🦾

reviews.review_description: I wasn't fortunate enough to play the first one but having this as my entry to the series was fantastic. I really wasnt sure what to expect diving in but boy was I impressed. There is a great tutorial that the game drops you into to showcase the weapons and playstyle. You get a hand cannon, pistol and some futuristic swords and axes. The cannon and pistol can be upgraded by completing levels/challenges. The game itself is a platformer mixed with an arcade shooter that may bring some nostalgia for those older gamers who have played Doom or Mega man. The graphics are smooth and each level has a unique environment and feel which is great to mix things up. The game is a bit challenging but offers check points for you to return to when you die so keep trying! very good game worth a try for any shooter fans.

I had an absolute blast playing this. Everything from the fast paced action to the difficulty of the levels and the insane weaponry u can get. 

Highly recommend it. If anyone knows me and my play style I’m all about high octane action & this game def brings the 🔥

Quick review, it’s fun to play once you get the hang of it, various world levels of different intensity and difficulty  plenty of replay value trying to beat your high scores , it’s definitely one I will keep coming back to when I want to have some fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks for Checking it out! hope you play it till the end!

reviews.review_description:
System Critical 2 is a nice follow-up to the incredibly difficult original. The worlds are huge and awe-inspiring, the enemies are ruthless, and the learning curve is very steep. The only notable drawback is the sound. I'm not a huge fan of the music and the sound effects could use a LOT of work, but this can be easily remedied by putting on some headphones with your own music. There's a ton of content for the price here, making it a must-add to the ol' library.

l i n k t r . e e / q u a c k b i r t h

System Critical is such a smooth running fast paced 
Gun totting experience, it just simply should not be this much fun.
I’m early into this game and it’s definitely a challenge, gun mechanics are amazing and very accurate.
The techno beat is stunning, I feel like dancing as I shoot my way through the levels.
I’m am yet to make it it to the boss fight but I’m not given up.
This is so much fun, you would be crazy to miss out on this game.
Excellent work by the solo developer Timothy.
Definitely give it a go, you will not be disappointed.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for checking it out! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
Amazing title, if you liked the first you’ll love the second.
If you were a gamer in the 80’s, 90’s and loved platformers, you’ll love this game. 
Flawless motion, satisfying unlimited ammo. Moving platforms, side rooms with boosts. A must try platformer in first person for 80’s nostalgia.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing! I really appreciate the support! 🙏

reviews.review_title: Promising Game but Hampered by Its Flaws
reviews.review_description:
Unfortunately, the experience did not meet my expectations. As a left-handed player, the lack of options to customize the controls was a huge obstacle. The inability to change the movement joystick makes the gameplay frustrating. Additionally, there are no options to switch from snap rotation to smooth rotation, which is a standard in VR games.

Air control when jumping is nearly nonexistent, leading to a lack of mastery over movements in a VR platformer where fluidity is essential. AI-controlled enemies are equally disappointing, charging straight at you while shooting, making combat repetitive. You quickly find yourself flailing in all directions to avoid shots without falling off the platforms, which becomes exhausting. The teleporting enemies could benefit from a visual effect to better signal their movement.

The level design is poor, with some effort made to create an atmosphere through decent 3D models, but some textures lack coherence. The use of AI-generated images, which is otherwise quite well integrated into the game, could have helped address these issues.

The music is a matter of taste, but the lack of an option to turn it off is a downside, as it can quickly become repetitive.

The developer seems to be working on a third installment despite the glaring shortcomings of this game. Why rush into a sequel without refining the current experience?

In summary, this game seems to have been designed with limited quality standards, justifying its shortcomings by a desire to offer a challenge, but at the expense of accessibility, gameplay fluidity, and the overall experience. Despite its present potential, this title suffers from major flaws that, if addressed, could easily elevate it. Unfortunately, in its current state, it remains a frustrating, repetitive, and unfinished experience, with an uncertain future if these issues are not resolved before moving on to another sequel.

developer_response.body: Sorry there are no left handed stick accommodations. You can definitely shoot and hold any weapon in your left hand. Also there is definitely an option to switch between snap and smooth turning just click in the right stick. Also there is definitely air control while platforming. The game features some of the best first person platforming VR has to offer. The levels are HUGE and super Cool not sure why you didn’t think so. 🤷‍♂️ I hope you play more and get to see all the levels and unlock some of the cool blasters. I’m sorry if the game was too hard for you or not your style, but it is a good game regardless! 🦾

reviews.review_description: That game gives me retro vibes but with a modern twist.
